title: Subscriber member ID missing
message: The subscriber's member identification number is missing from the subscriber name loop.
why: Without a member ID the payer cannot match the claim to a beneficiary; the claim rejects at the front-end edits before adjudication.
fix: Enter the subscriber's Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) exactly as it appears on the member's card.

title: Subscriber name or date of birth missing
message: The subscriber's last name, first name, or date of birth is missing.
why: Beneficiary matching requires full name and birth date; incomplete demographics cause a front-end rejection.
fix: Complete the subscriber's last name, first name, and date of birth in the subscriber loop.

title: Payer identification missing
message: The destination payer's name or payer ID is missing.
why: The clearinghouse cannot route a claim without a payer identifier; the transaction is returned unprocessed.
fix: Select the destination payer so the payer name and payer ID populate the payer name loop.

title: Billing provider NPI missing
message: The billing provider's National Provider Identifier is missing.
why: The billing NPI is a required identifier on every professional claim; its absence is an automatic rejection.
fix: Enter the organization's Type 2 (organizational) NPI in the billing provider loop.

title: Billing provider tax ID missing
message: The billing provider's employer identification number (TIN) is missing.
why: Payment and 1099 reporting are keyed to the billing TIN; claims without one cannot be adjudicated.
fix: Enter the billing organization's EIN in the billing provider tax identification reference.

title: Billing provider taxonomy missing
message: The billing provider specialty (taxonomy) code is missing.
why: The taxonomy code identifies the billing specialty used for pricing and edits; many payers reject claims without it.
fix: Enter the billing provider's 10-character taxonomy code in the billing specialty segment.

title: Billing provider address incomplete
message: The billing provider's street address, city, state, or ZIP code is missing.
why: A complete practice location address is required for claim routing, pricing locality, and payment.
fix: Complete the billing provider street address, city, state, and ZIP code.

title: Rendering provider NPI missing
message: The rendering provider's National Provider Identifier is missing.
why: The payer must know which clinician performed the service; a missing rendering NPI fails front-end edits.
fix: Enter the rendering clinician's Type 1 (individual) NPI in the rendering provider loop.

title: No diagnosis codes on claim
message: The claim contains no diagnosis codes.
why: At least one ICD-10-CM diagnosis is required to establish why services were performed; a claim without diagnoses rejects immediately.
fix: Add at least one ICD-10-CM diagnosis code supported by the encounter documentation.

title: No service lines on claim
message: The claim contains no service lines.
why: A professional claim must bill at least one service; an empty claim cannot be processed.
fix: Add at least one service line with a procedure code, charge, units, and date of service.

title: Claim total does not equal sum of line charges
message: The claim-level total charge does not equal the sum of the service line charges.
why: Balancing edits compare the claim total to the line totals; a mismatch is an automatic front-end rejection.
fix: Recalculate the claim total so it equals the sum of all service line charges.

title: Invalid diagnosis pointer
message: A service line references a diagnosis position that does not exist on the claim.
why: Diagnosis pointers must reference positions 1-12 of the submitted diagnosis list; a dangling pointer makes the line unadjudicable.
fix: Correct the line's diagnosis pointers so each one references a diagnosis actually present on the claim.

title: Duplicate service line
message: Two or more service lines bill the same procedure with the same modifiers on the same date of service.
why: Exact duplicate lines trigger duplicate-billing edits and can flag the claim for integrity review.
fix: Remove the duplicate line, or add an appropriate modifier and documentation if the service was genuinely repeated.

title: Invalid claim frequency or missing original claim reference
message: The claim frequency code must be 1 (original), 7 (replacement), or 8 (void); frequency 7 or 8 also requires the original claim number.
why: Replacement and void claims must reference the claim being corrected; without it the payer cannot link the adjustment.
fix: Use frequency code 1 for a first submission, or supply the original claim number when submitting a replacement (7) or void (8).

title: Patient control number missing
message: The patient control number (claim submitter identifier) is missing.
why: The patient control number is echoed back on the remittance; without it payments cannot be reconciled to the claim.
fix: Assign a unique patient control number to the claim.

title: Invalid ICD-10-CM code format
message: A diagnosis code does not match the ICD-10-CM code format.
why: Malformed diagnosis codes fail code-set validation and reject the claim.
fix: Correct the diagnosis to a valid ICD-10-CM code (letter, two characters, optional decimal extension), coded to the highest specificity.

title: Invalid procedure code format
message: A service line procedure code does not match the CPT/HCPCS code format.
why: Procedure codes must be five characters (CPT: four digits plus a digit or letter; HCPCS: a letter plus four digits); malformed codes fail code-set validation.
fix: Correct the procedure code to a valid five-character CPT or HCPCS code.

title: NPI fails check-digit validation
message: A provider NPI is not a valid 10-digit identifier (fails the check-digit calculation).
why: NPIs carry a Luhn check digit; an NPI that fails the check cannot belong to any enrolled provider and rejects the claim.
fix: Verify the NPI against the provider's enrollment record and re-enter all 10 digits.

title: Provider not found in payer registry
message: A provider NPI on the claim is not present in the payer's enrolled-provider registry.
why: Medicare pays only enrolled providers; claims from unrecognized NPIs are denied for provider enrollment.
fix: Confirm the provider's enrollment with the payer, or correct the NPI to the enrolled provider who performed the service.

title: Date of service outside documented encounter
message: A service line's date of service falls outside the documented encounter period.
why: Services billed on dates with no corresponding documented visit are unsupported and denied on review.
fix: Correct the line date of service to match the documented encounter date.

title: Date of service in the future
message: A service line's date of service is after today's date.
why: Claims cannot bill services that have not yet occurred; future dates reject at front-end edits.
fix: Correct the date of service to the actual date the service was performed.

title: Place of service inconsistent with encounter setting
message: The place of service code is invalid or inconsistent with the documented encounter setting.
why: POS drives pricing and coverage rules; an office POS on an inpatient stay (or vice versa) causes denials or incorrect payment and is an audit flag.
fix: Set the place of service to match the documented setting (e.g., 11 office for ambulatory, 21 for inpatient, 12 for home).

title: Invalid units or charge
message: A service line has units below 1 or a negative charge amount.
why: Units must be at least 1 and charges non-negative for the line to price; invalid values reject the line.
fix: Correct the line's unit count and charge amount.

title: Invalid modifier format
message: A procedure modifier is not a two-character alphanumeric value.
why: Modifiers must be exactly two characters; malformed modifiers fail code-set validation.
fix: Correct the modifier to a valid two-character value or remove it.

title: No active eligibility on date of service
message: The eligibility file shows no active coverage for this member on the date of service.
why: Claims for members without active coverage on the DOS are denied for eligibility.
fix: Verify the member's coverage dates; bill the payer that was active on the date of service.

title: Subscriber ID does not match eligibility file
message: The submitted member ID does not match the payer's eligibility record for this patient.
why: An ID/patient mismatch causes a beneficiary-match rejection even when the patient is covered.
fix: Correct the member ID to the identifier on the patient's eligibility record with this payer.

title: Claim past timely filing limit
message: The claim is being submitted after the payer's timely filing window measured from the date of service.
why: Claims received after the filing deadline are denied and the balance generally cannot be billed to the patient.
fix: Submit within the payer's filing window, or attach documentation supporting a timely-filing exception.

title: Subscriber demographics do not match chart
message: The subscriber's date of birth or gender on the claim differs from the patient chart.
why: Demographic mismatches cause beneficiary-match failures and can indicate the wrong patient was selected.
fix: Reconcile the claim demographics with the patient chart and the payer's records.

title: Billed procedure not supported by documentation
message: A billed procedure has no supporting documentation in the encounter note, transcript, or structured record.
why: Services must be documented to be payable; billing undocumented services is denied on medical review and is a program-integrity risk.
fix: Remove the unsupported line, or ensure the performed service is documented before billing.

title: Diagnosis not supported by documentation
message: A submitted diagnosis is not documented in the encounter note, transcript, or problem list.
why: Diagnoses must reflect documented conditions; unsupported diagnoses constitute miscoding and can inflate risk scores improperly.
fix: Remove the unsupported diagnosis or correct it to the condition actually documented.

title: E/M level not supported by documented time or complexity
message: The billed evaluation and management level exceeds what the documented visit time or medical decision-making supports.
why: E/M levels are payable only at the documented intensity; upcoding is a leading denial and audit driver.
fix: Rebill at the E/M level supported by the documented time or complexity.

title: Screening billed without documented result
message: A screening service is billed but no screening instrument result is documented for the visit.
why: Screening services require a documented administration and result to be payable.
fix: Document the screening tool and score, or remove the screening line.

title: Service linked to unrelated diagnosis
message: A service line's primary diagnosis pointer references a diagnosis unrelated to the service performed.
why: The pointed diagnosis establishes medical necessity for the line; an unrelated linkage causes necessity denials.
fix: Point the line to the documented diagnosis that justifies the service.

title: Medical necessity not established
message: The documentation does not establish why the billed service was clinically necessary for this patient on this date.
why: Payers cover only reasonable and necessary services; claims lacking a documented indication are denied on review.
fix: Ensure the note records the indication for the service, or do not bill it.
